## Project Structure & Overview

**Runwise** is a full-stack React + Node.js/Express web application for runners. It's a **PWA (Progressive Web App)** that integrates with Strava for workout tracking and includes an AI chat coach powered by DeepSeek API.

### Key Technologies:
- **Frontend**: React 18 + TypeScript, React-i18n (multilingual), Recharts (charting), PWA support
- **Backend**: Node.js/Express, JWT auth, Supabase (PostgreSQL), Strava OAuth, DeepSeek AI API
- **Languages**: Russian, English, Ukrainian (auto-detected from browser)

---

## AI Chat UI Files Overview

### Core Chat Screen
**`/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/screens/AIChat.tsx`** (505 lines)
- Main chat interface component
- Handles message state management (user/AI/system messages)
- Implements **streaming chat** via SSE (Server-Sent Events)
- Manages chat history loading and storage
- Controls **scroll behavior** with auto-scroll to bottom
- Implements **user scroll detection** (doesn't auto-scroll if user scrolled up)
- Settings modal for AI preferences (gender, personality, response length, emoji level)
- Daily message limit tracking and display
- Plan update notifications when AI modifies weekly/macro plans

**Key Features**:
- Message interface with `id`, `role` (user/ai/system), `content`, `timestamp`
- Refs for scroll management:
  - `messagesEndRef` - Target for scrollIntoView
  - `messagesContainerRef` - Main messages container
  - `userScrolledUp` - Flag to detect manual scroll
  - `scrollRestoredRef` - Tracks when history loads
- Loading indicators: typing animation, thinking spinner, plan updating spinner
- Input field with send button, disabled when loading or limit reached
- Portal-based modal for AI settings with animations

### Chat Styling
**`/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/screens/AIChat.css`** (394 lines)

**Critical Layout Classes**:

| Class | Purpose | Key CSS |
|-------|---------|---------|
| `.ai-chat-screen` | Main container | `height: calc(100vh - 68px)`, `overflow: hidden`, `flex-direction: column`, fixed height minus nav bar |
| `.chat-header` | Title + settings button | `flex-shrink: 0`, no scroll |
| `.chat-container` | Flex wrapper | `flex: 1`, `flex-direction: column`, `min-height: 0` (critical for flex child) |
| `.messages` | Messages scroll area | `flex: 1`, `overflow-y: auto`, `-webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch`, `scrollbar-width: none` (hides scrollbar), `gap: 16px` |
| `.message` | Individual message wrapper | `animation: slideIn 0.3s`, flex direction varies |
| `.message.user-message` | User bubble | `justify-content: flex-end` |
| `.message.ai-message` | AI bubble | `justify-content: flex-start` |
| `.message-content` | Message bubble styling | `max-width: 80%`, `padding: 16px`, `border-radius: 12px`, different bg colors per role |
| `.chat-input-area` | **Fixed positioned input** | **`position: fixed`, `bottom: 68px`**, `left: 50%`, `transform: translateX(-50%)`, `width: 100%`, `max-width: 420px`, `z-index: 50`, `background-color: var(--color-bg)` |
| `.chat-input-wrapper` | Input + button container | `display: flex`, `gap: 8px`, `border: 1px solid var(--color-border)` |
| `.chat-input` | Text input | `flex: 1`, `border: none`, `color: var(--color-text)`, font-size 14px |

**Scroll & Overflow Behavior**:
- `.messages::-webkit-scrollbar` - HIDDEN (display: none)
- `-webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch` - Smooth momentum scrolling on iOS
- `scrollbar-width: none` - Hides scrollbar on Firefox
- Messages container has `flex: 1` with `overflow-y: auto` for internal scroll

**Important Positioning**:
- Input area is **FIXED** at bottom (68px above for nav bar space)
- Messages container must have `min-height: 0` for flex child to work
- Z-index: 50 keeps input above content

**Animations**:
- `.message` slides in: `messageSlide` or `messageSlideRight` (0.25s)
- `.typing-indicator` - Three bouncing dots (opacity + translateY)
- `.thinking-spinner` - Rotating border spinner (0.8s)
- `.plan-updating-spinner` - Rotating border spinner (0.8s)

**Colors & Styling**:
- User message: `var(--color-accent)` (#3b82f6 blue) with white text
- AI message: `var(--color-bg-secondary)` with border
- System message: Green background `rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.15)`
- Plan update: Yellow background `rgba(234, 179, 8, 0.15)`
- Thinking: Blue `#60a5fa`

### Chat Message Component
**`/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/components/ChatMessage.tsx`** (69 lines)
- Renders individual chat messages
- **Parses inline workout links** with regex: `[text](workout:UUID)`
- Renders workout links as clickable buttons with class `.workout-link`
- Different rendering based on message role (user/ai/system)

**Styling for `.workout-link`**:
- `color: #60a5fa` (blue)
- `text-decoration: underline`
- No background/border
- Clickable cursor

---

## Global Layout & Container Styling

### App Layout
**`/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/App.css`** (759 lines)

**Key Layout Classes**:

| Class | Purpose | CSS Details |
|-------|---------|----------|
| `.app` | Root container | `display: flex`, `flex-direction: column`, `height: 100vh`, `width: 100%` |
| `.app-container` | Main content area | `flex: 1`, `overflow-y: auto`, `padding-bottom: 68px` (nav bar space), scrollbar hidden, `scrollbar-gutter: stable` |
| `.screen` | Any screen component | `padding: 24px`, `animation: slideIn 0.3s ease-out` |
| `.bottom-nav` | Navigation bar | **`position: fixed`, `bottom: 0`**, `height: 60px`, `z-index: 100`, flexbox with 5 nav items |

**Critical for Chat**:
- App container scrolls, individual screens don't (except AIChat which has internal scroll)
- 68px bottom padding for nav bar (60px nav + 8px gap)
- Z-index hierarchy: nav (100) > chat input (50) > content (auto)

### Root & Global Styles
**`/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/index.css`** (72 lines)

**Global Settings**:
```css
html, body, #root { width: 100%, height: 100%, overflow: hidden }
body { font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto... }
::-webkit-scrollbar { width: 4px, background: transparent }
::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb { background: #2a3a52 }
```

**Design Tokens** (CSS variables):
- Colors: `--color-bg` (#0f1419), `--color-bg-secondary` (#1a2235), `--color-accent` (#3b82f6)
- Spacing: `--spacing-sm` (8px), `--spacing-md` (16px), `--spacing-lg` (24px)
- Radius: `--radius-md` (8px), `--radius-lg` (12px)

---

## API Integration

**`/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/api/api.ts`** (253 lines)

**Chat-Related API Methods**:

```typescript
export const ai = {
  // Streaming chat with SSE
  chatStream: async (
    message: string,
    onChunk: (text: string) => void,  // Called for each streamed chunk
    onDone: (meta: {...}) => void,     // Called when complete with metadata
    onThinking?: () => void             // Called when AI enters thinking mode
  ): Promise<void>
  
  // Regular (non-streaming) chat
  chat: (message: string) => request('/api/ai/chat', {...})
  
  // Chat management
  chatLimit: () => request('/api/ai/chat/limit')      // Get daily limit info
  chatHistory: () => request('/api/ai/chat/history')  // Load message history
  clearChatHistory: () => request('/api/ai/chat/history', { method: 'DELETE' })
  
  // AI personality preferences
  // Saved to profile, cached in localStorage
  AiPreferences {
    coach_gender: 'male' | 'female'
    response_length: 'short' | 'medium' | 'long'
    personality: 'bro' | 'strict' | 'calm' | 'motivator'
    humor: boolean
    emoji_level: 'few' | 'many'
  }
}
```

---

## Backend Chat Routes

**`/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/server/routes/ai/`**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `chat.js` | Chat streaming endpoint, history retrieval, limit checking |
| `context.js` | Loads user data (workouts, goals, metrics) for AI context |
| `deepseek.js` | DeepSeek API integration, streaming handling |
| `prompts.js` | System prompts, personality templates, formatting rules (115KB!) |
| `plan.js` | Weekly plan generation and modification |
| `macroPlan.js` | Macro plan (annual) handling |
| `tools.js` | AI tools (search workouts, get stats, etc.) |
| `zones.js` | Pace zones calculation |

---

## File Paths Summary

### Chat UI Files (React Components)
1. `/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/screens/AIChat.tsx` - Main chat screen
2. `/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/screens/AIChat.css` - Chat styling
3. `/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/components/ChatMessage.tsx` - Message renderer

### Styling Files
4. `/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/App.css` - Global layout (screens, nav, modals, animations)
5. `/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/index.css` - Root styles (theme, typography)

### API Integration
6. `/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/api/api.ts` - AI chat API methods

### Backend Implementation
7. `/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/server/routes/ai/chat.js` - Chat endpoint
8. `/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/server/routes/ai/deepseek.js` - AI streaming
9. `/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/server/routes/ai/context.js` - Context loading

---

## Key Scroll Behavior Details

**The chat implements smart scroll behavior**:
1. **Auto-scroll on new messages** - When user is at bottom (within 100px threshold)
2. **Manual scroll detection** - `distanceFromBottom > 100px` = user scrolled up
3. **History load scroll** - Always scroll to bottom when chat history loads
4. **Tab switch scroll** - Re-scroll when tab becomes active (visibility change)
5. **Momentum scrolling** - `-webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch` for iOS

**Height Calculation**: 
- AIChat screen: `100vh - 68px` (minus nav bar)
- Messages container: `flex: 1` with `overflow-y: auto`
- Input area: `position: fixed` at `bottom: 68px`

This structure ensures the input stays always visible at the bottom while messages scroll within the available space.

content.thinking:
Now I understand the problem. The AI chat has a fixed-positioned input area at `bottom: 68px`, and the messages container uses `flex: 1` with `overflow-y: auto`. The issue is that messages are being cut off at the bottom - the user can't scroll down far enough to see the full AI response because part of it is hidden behind the input field.

Let me read the actual CSS and TSX files to understand the exact current state.

content.text: Понял проблему — сообщения в чате обрезаются снизу, часть текста прячется за полем ввода и ниже прокрутить нельзя. Давай посмотрю текущий код.

Вижу проблему. `chat-input-area` имеет `position: fixed` и позиционируется на `bottom: 68px`, но при этом контейнер `.messages` не учитывает высоту этого поля ввода — он просто занимает всё доступное пространство через `flex: 1`, а последние сообщения прячутся за фиксированным полем ввода.

Нужно добавить `padding-bottom` к `.messages`, чтобы можно было прокрутить контент ниже фиксированного поля ввода. Также нужно убрать `chat-input-area` из потока `chat-container`, т.к. сейчас он и fixed, и внутри flex-контейнера — это создаёт конфликт.

Давай посмотрю на `App.css` чтобы уточнить как `app-container` взаимодействует с этим.

Теперь я вижу полную картину. Проблема в том, что:

1. `.chat-input-area` — `position: fixed`, но находится **внутри** flex-контейнера `.chat-container`. Хотя `fixed` элемент не занимает место в потоке, он визуально перекрывает нижнюю часть `.messages`.
2. У `.messages` нет `padding-bottom`, поэтому последние сообщения прячутся за полем ввода и прокрутить дальше нельзя.

Решение: добавить `padding-bottom` к `.messages`, равный примерной высоте поля ввода (~70px), чтобы контент мог прокрутиться выше фиксированного input.

content.text:
Теперь давай также проверим — `quick-questions` блок тоже может добавлять высоту к input area. Убедимся, что 80px достаточно. Значит, высота input area — это примерно padding (6px) + input-wrapper (~44px) + возможный limit-banner. 80px должно хватить с запасом.

---

**Что сделал:**

1. **`AIChat.css`** — добавил `padding-bottom: 80px` к `.messages`. Это создаёт пустое пространство внизу области сообщений, позволяя прокрутить контент выше фиксированного поля ввода. Без этого последние сообщения прятались за input и ниже прокрутить было невозможно.

**Суть фикса:** контейнер сообщений теперь имеет нижний отступ 80px, равный высоте поля ввода. Это позволяет прокрутить все сообщения полностью, даже последние — они больше не прячутся за фиксированным полем ввода.

Perfect! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create a comprehensive summary of the race prediction system:

## Summary of Race Predictions/Forecasts in Runwise

I've found all the race prediction code in this project. Here's a comprehensive breakdown:

### **1. Core Prediction Logic - SERVER**

**File: `/server/routes/workouts/predictions.js` (lines 1-382)**

This is the main endpoint: `GET /api/workouts/goals/predictions`

#### **Data Sources for Predictions:**

**A. Riegel Formula (Riegel-based estimation)**
- **Source:** `best_efforts` field from workouts (Strava-provided splits)
- **Formula:** `rTime = e.moving_time * Math.pow(targetDistM / eDist, riegelExp)`
- **Time Window:** Last 4 weeks (28 days)
- **Best Effort Distances Used:**
  - 1K (1000m), 1 Mile (1609m), 2 Mile (3219m)
  - 5K (5000m), 10K (10000m)
  - Half-Marathon (21097m), Marathon (42195m)

**B. Strava Splits (Best Efforts)**
- **Source:** `best_efforts` from Strava workout data
- **Target Splits:** Exact distance match (5K, 10K, Half-Marathon, Marathon)
- **Processing:** Looks for the best (fastest) time for the target distance in the last 4 weeks

#### **Prediction Calculation Algorithm (for PB goals - pb_5k, pb_10k, pb_21k, pb_42k):**

**Step 1: Riegel Baseline Calculation**
- **Time Window:** Last 4 weeks only
- **Adaptive Riegel Exponent** (based on recent average pace):
  - `avgPace < 4.5 min/km → exponent 1.06` (fast runners)
  - `avgPace < 5.5 min/km → exponent 1.08`
  - `avgPace < 6.5 min/km → exponent 1.10`
  - `avgPace ≥ 6.5 min/km → exponent 1.12` (slower runners)
- **Freshness Weighting:** `freshness = max(0.5, 1 - daysAgo/28 * 0.3)`
  - Recent workouts (0 days ago) → weight 1.0
  - 28 days ago → weight 0.7
  - Older than 28 days → weight 0.5
- **Output:** Weighted median of top-3 Riegel estimates (sorted by freshness-weighted time)

**Step 2: Strava Best Effort Lookup**
- Find the best time for the exact target distance from last 4 weeks
- **Sanity Check:** If best effort is >15% faster than Riegel median:
  - Mark as GPS glitch and discard
  - Example: "На 20% быстрее расчёта — вероятно GPS-глюк"

**Step 3: Final Prediction Selection**
- **Priority 1:** If both exist: Use whichever is faster (Strava or Riegel)
- **Priority 2:** If only Strava split exists: Use Strava
- **Priority 3:** If only Riegel exists: Use Riegel
- **Fallback:** If neither exists: Return "Нет тренировок за 4 недели"

#### **Other Goal Types:**
- **monthly_distance:** Extrapolated based on current month + remaining days
- **weekly_distance:** Extrapolated based on current week + remaining days
- **monthly_runs:** Extrapolated based on runs completed + remaining days

#### **Suspicious Workout Handling:**
- Workouts marked as `is_suspicious` and not user-verified are excluded
- Uses manual distance/time if available in `manual_distance` and `manual_moving_time` fields

### **2. Client-Side Implementation**

**File: `/client/src/screens/profile/GoalsSection.tsx` (lines 25-406)**

- Fetches predictions via `workouts.goalPredictions()` API call
- Displays breakdown modal showing:
  - **Итог** (Result/Summary) - the final chosen source
  - **Strava-сплит** - best effort from Strava (if found)
  - **Расчёты Ригеля** - detailed Riegel calculations from last 4 weeks
  - Discarded splits with GPS glitch reason
- **Top-3 Highlighting:** Shows top 3 Riegel workout entries used for median calculation

**File: `/client/src/api/api.ts` (line 110)**
- API call: `goalPredictions: () => request('/api/workouts/goals/predictions')`

### **3. Localization Strings**

**File: `/client/src/i18n/locales/ru.json`**

```
"forecast": "прогноз"
"breakdownTitle": "Разбор прогноза"
"breakdownResult": "Итог"
"breakdownStravaSplit": "Strava-сплит ({{dist}} км)"
"breakdownDiscarded": "Отсечённый сплит"
"breakdownRiegel": "Расчёты Ригеля (последние {{period}})"
"breakdownNoData": "Нет данных для расчёта"
"breakdownTop3": "Топ-3 выделены — из них берётся медиана"
```

### **4. Data Persistence**

**File: `/server/routes/workouts/goals.js`**
- Goals table stores: `type`, `target_value`, `deadline`, `current_value`
- **Important:** `predicted_time` field is updated by the predictions endpoint after calculation (line 327-333):
  ```javascript
  supabase.from('goals').update({ predicted_time: finalTime }).eq('id', goal.id).then(() => {});
  ```

### **5. Splits and Best Efforts Data Source**

**File: `/server/routes/strava.js`**

**1km Splits (from Strava):**
- Fetched via `/api/strava/sync-splits/:workoutId`
- Stored in `splits` field
- Strava provides `splits_metric` array with `moving_time` per kilometer

**500m Splits (calculated from GPS streams):**
- Endpoint: `POST /api/strava/sync-splits-500/:workoutId`
- Calculated from distance/time/heartrate streams using interpolation
- Stored in `splits_500m` field

**Best Efforts:**
- Fetched with workout details from Strava API
- Stored as JSON in `best_efforts` field
- Contains: `name`, `distance`, `moving_time`

### **6. VDOT Integration**

**File: `/server/routes/ai/vdot.js`**

While not directly used for race predictions, VDOT can inform them:
- Used for pace zone calculations
- Can estimate training zones based on best recent quality workout
- VDOT calculation uses Daniels-Gilbert formula
- Quality workout types: 'race', 'tempo', 'interval', 'fartlek', 'long'

### **7. AI Context Integration**

**File: `/server/routes/ai/context.js` (lines 263-290)**

`getRiegelPredictions()` function reads stored predictions for AI chat context:
- Maps goals with predicted_time field
- Formats for AI prompts showing gap from target time
- Used in chat system to discuss realistic goal achievement

**File: `/server/routes/ai/prompts.js` (lines 94-101)**

`formatPredictionsForAI()` formats predictions with:
- Headers: "ПРОГНОЗЫ ВРЕМЕНИ (расчёт Ригеля по недавним тренировкам)"
- Shows gap in minutes/seconds compared to target
- Visual indicators (✅ faster, ⚠️ slower)

### **Key Points Summary:**

1. **Time Window:** Only last 4 weeks of workouts are used
2. **Formula:** Riegel running formula with adaptive exponent based on current pace
3. **Data Sources:** Strava best efforts + calculated Riegel from shorter distances
4. **Freshness Weighting:** More recent workouts weighted more heavily
5. **Quality Check:** GPS anomalies excluded; >15% faster splits discarded as glitches
6. **Update Frequency:** Predictions calculated on-demand when `/api/workouts/goals/predictions` is called
7. **Ranking:** Weighted median of top-3 freshness-weighted Riegel estimates
8. **Display:** Shows full breakdown with all source workouts and reasoning for the chosen method

Вот как работает система прогнозов:

**Окно данных — последние 4 недели.** Прогноз строится только по тренировкам за 28 дней. Твой полумарафон с результатом 1:38:04 попал в это окно как Strava best effort.

**Логика выбора:** система сравнивает два источника — твой реальный Strava-сплит (1:38:04) и расчёт по формуле Ригеля из более коротких дистанций (1:48:26). Берётся более быстрый результат, если он не отличается больше чем на 15% (иначе считается GPS-глюком). Твой результат быстрее, но в допустимых рамках — поэтому он и стоит как итоговый прогноз.

**Когда изменится:**
- Прогноз пересчитывается **каждый раз** при открытии экрана с целями (запрос `/api/workouts/goals/predictions`)
- Через **~4 недели** после полумарафона этот забег выйдет из окна 28 дней, и прогноз переключится на расчёт Ригеля по другим тренировкам
- Если за это время ты пробежишь ещё какие-то качественные тренировки — Ригель-расчёт обновится на основе новых данных

**Кратко:** прогноз 1:38:04 будет стоять ~4 недели с момента забега, потом пропадёт и останется только Ригель-расчёт.

---

**Что сделал:** исследовал всю систему прогнозов — серверный файл `predictions.js`, клиентский `GoalsSection.tsx`, API и логику расчётов. Объяснил механику: окно 4 недели, приоритет Strava-сплита над Ригелем, автообновление при каждом запросе.

content.thinking:
The user is asking me to brainstorm ideas for integrating heart rate (HR) data into their running app. They want it to be optional - just enhancing existing features. Let me think about this from a running science perspective and what their app already has.

Let me think about what the app already has:
1. VDOT calculations
2. Riegel-based race predictions
3. Pace zones
4. AI coach chat
5. Training plans (weekly/macro)
6. Strava integration (which already provides HR data)

Key areas where HR can add value:


HR data could refine VDOT estimates by validating whether a given pace truly reflects fitness level—if someone hits a tempo run at target pace but stays in an easy HR zone, they're probably fitter than the pace suggests. I could also layer in training load metrics like TRIMP to quantify training stress and feed that into fatigue models. Finally, resting HR trends and recovery patterns would give insight into race readiness and overall fatigue accumulation.

Since they already pull HR streams from Strava, the most practical wins would be validating easy-run paces against HR zones and using HR:pace ratios to track aerobic efficiency over time. I should frame this as ideas to explore rather than a concrete roadmap—curious what resonates with their current setup and what they think would be most useful.

content.text:
Хороший вопрос. У вас уже есть HR-данные из Strava (потоки heartrate приходят при синке сплитов), так что инфраструктура готова. Вот что реально полезно и органично ляжет на существующие фичи:

---

### 1. HR-зоны + уточнение VDOT

Сейчас VDOT считается чисто по темпу. Но одна и та же тренировка на пульсе 150 и 175 — это разный уровень формы. Можно:

- **Cardiac Efficiency (CE)** — отношение темпа к пульсу. Если пользователь бежит 5:00/км на пульсе 145, а через месяц те же 5:00 на 138 — форма растёт, даже если темп не изменился
- **Корректировка VDOT:** если средний HR на качественной тренировке был ниже ожидаемого для этой зоны — можно слегка поднять VDOT-оценку (и наоборот)

### 2. Прогнозы с учётом пульса

Сейчас Ригель считает только по времени/дистанции. Но если полумарафон пробежан на пульсе 185 (почти максимум) — резервов мало, прогноз реалистичен. А если на 165 — значит человек бежал "в полноги" и реальный потенциал выше. Можно добавить **HR-коэффициент** к прогнозу:

- `%HRmax` или `%HRR` на забеге → множитель к Ригелю
- Это мягкая коррекция, не ломает текущую логику

### 3. Aerobic Decoupling (дрейф пульса)

На длинных пробежках — сравнить пульс первой и второй половины при одинаковом темпе. Если drift < 5% — аэробная база в порядке. Если > 10% — длинные рано усложнять. Это:

- Показывать как метрику в карточке тренировки
- Передавать в AI-контекст, чтобы тренер мог советовать

### 4. AI-тренер с пульсовым контекстом

Самый простой и мощный вариант — просто **добавить HR-данные в контекст для AI**:

- Средний/макс пульс по тренировкам
- Пульс по зонам (сколько времени в каждой)
- Тренды CE за последние недели

AI-тренер сможет говорить: "Ты бежал лёгкую на пульсе 170 — это слишком высоко, сбавь темп" или "Твой пульс на темповых снизился — форма растёт".

### 5. Оценка восстановления

- **TRIMP** (Training Impulse) — нагрузка с учётом пульса и длительности. Проще чем TSS, но эффективно
- Показывать простой индикатор "нагрузка за неделю" — низкая/средняя/высокая
- AI может учитывать при корректировке плана

---

### Что я бы сделал в первую очередь

По соотношению усилие/польза:

| Приоритет | Фича | Почему |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | HR в AI-контексте | Минимум кода, максимум пользы — тренер сразу станет умнее |
| 2 | Cardiac Efficiency (тренд) | Простой расчёт, наглядный график роста формы |
| 3 | HR-коррекция прогнозов | Улучшает точность, но нужна калибровка |
| 4 | Aerobic Decoupling | Полезно для длинных, но нишевая метрика |
| 5 | TRIMP / нагрузка | Требует больше UI-работы |

Всё это опционально — если у пользователя нет HR-датчика, всё работает как раньше. Просто при наличии данных — точнее и информативнее.

Что из этого интересно — могу детальнее расписать или начать реализовывать.

---

**Что сделал:** проанализировал текущую архитектуру (VDOT, Ригель, AI-контекст, Strava-данные) и предложил 5 направлений интеграции пульса — от простого (HR в AI-контекст) до продвинутого (TRIMP). Всё опционально и не ломает существующий функционал.

input.prompt:
Explore the heart rate (HR) data flow in this Runwise project. I need to understand:

1. **How HR data comes from Strava**: Look at `/server/routes/strava.js` - how are heartrate streams fetched, stored? What fields in the database hold HR data?
2. **What HR fields exist in workout records**: Check the database schema or any Supabase references for HR-related columns (average_heartrate, max_heartrate, heartrate_zones, etc.)
3. **Splits data**: Look at how splits are stored - do 500m splits already include HR data? Check the sync-splits endpoints.
4. **VDOT calculation**: Read `/server/routes/ai/vdot.js` thoroughly - understand the full algorithm, what inputs it takes, and where it's used.
5. **AI context**: Read `/server/routes/ai/context.js` - what workout data is already passed to the AI? Is any HR data already included?
6. **Predictions**: Read `/server/routes/workouts/predictions.js` - full algorithm, understand where HR could be injected.
7. **Profile/settings**: Check if there's any place where user stores max HR or resting HR.

Be very thorough - read full files, not just snippets. Report exact file paths, line numbers, existing field names, and data structures.

Explore the client-side UI of the Runwise project related to:

1. **Workout detail/card**: Find all components that display workout information. Look for workout cards, workout detail screens, any existing HR display. Check files in `/client/src/screens/` and `/client/src/components/`.
2. **Profile/Goals section**: Read `/client/src/screens/profile/GoalsSection.tsx` - how predictions are displayed, what the breakdown modal shows.
3. **Training zones display**: Is there any current UI for pace zones or training zones? Check for any zone-related components.

Report all relevant file paths, component names, existing data fields displayed, and any existing HR-related UI elements.

content.text:
Perfect! Now let me compile the comprehensive report based on my exploration.

## Runwise Client-Side UI Exploration Report

I've thoroughly explored the Runwise client-side UI structure. Here's the complete breakdown:

### 1. **Workout Detail/Card Components**

**Primary Files:**
- `/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/screens/WorkoutDetail.tsx` - Main detailed workout view
- `/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/components/WorkoutRow.tsx` - Compact workout list item

**Existing Data Fields Displayed:**
- **Basic Info**: Name, type (badge), date (full format), description
- **Metrics Grid**:
  - Distance (manual_distance or distance with edit indicator)
  - Pace (calculated from time/distance)
  - Time (moving_time with edit indicator)
  - Heart Rate (average_heartrate and max_heartrate)
- **GPS/Data Quality**: Suspicious workout indicators and verification badges
- **Splits**: 
  - 1km splits with pace visualization (bar chart)
  - 500m splits (lazy-loaded on demand)
  - Heart rate per split (heartrate field in Split interface)
  - Fastest/slowest/average pace summary
- **AI Analysis**: Clickable analysis block

**Split Interface Structure** (line 35-48):
```
interface Split {
  km?: number;
  time?: number;
  pace?: number;
  distance?: number;
  heartrate?: number | null;  // HR data per split
  elevation?: number;
}
```

**HR-Related UI Elements:**
- Average HR metric card (line 318-324)
- Max HR sub-label
- HR per split visualization in splits section
- HR present in stats API responses

---

### 2. **Profile/Goals Section**

**File**: `/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/screens/profile/GoalsSection.tsx`

**Goal Types Supported**:
- `monthly_distance` / `weekly_distance` (distance input)
- `pb_5k` / `pb_10k` / `pb_21k` / `pb_42k` (time input)
- `monthly_runs` (number input)

**Breakdown Modal Displays** (line 339-398):
- **Chosen Result**: Source, reason, target distance
- **Best Effort Section**: Date and time for specific distance from Strava
- **Discarded BE**: Alternative best effort with reason for rejection
- **Riegel Calculations**: 
  - Table of workouts with date, distance, actual time, projected time
  - Shows top 3 workouts used
  - Displays Riegel exponent and avg pace
- **Stats**: Weekly KM volume, workouts analyzed count
- **Records Breakdown**: VDOT values per distance type with dates

**Progress Visualization**:
- Current vs target values
- Forecast calculation from predictions
- Animated progress bar with percentage
- Prediction messages (on-track indicators)
- Deadline status (passed/soon/ok with days remaining)

---

### 3. **Training Zones Display**

**File**: `/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/screens/profile/PaceZonesSection.tsx`

**UI Components**:
- **Summary Card** (line 137-154):
  - VDOT score
  - Level (beginner/intermediate/advanced)
  - Easy zone upper bound
  - Threshold zone upper bound

- **Full Zones Detail Modal** (line 164-289):
  - 5 zone types with color-coded indicators:
    - Easy (#4CAF50)
    - Marathon (#2196F3)
    - Threshold (#FF9800)
    - Interval (#f44336)
    - Repetition (#9C27B0)
  - Each zone shows: from–to pace range

- **Source Workout Info**:
  - Workout name (clickable link)
  - Date
  - Distance and moving time
  - Badge: "Recent" or "Decay" status
  - Decay details if applicable (original VDOT, decayed VDOT, age in days)

- **Other Good Workouts**: List with VDOT, distance, time, date

- **Stats Section**:
  - Weekly volume (km/week)
  - Workouts analyzed count
  - Per-record VDOT breakdown

- **Formula Explanation**: Localized text explaining calculation

---

### 4. **Stats/Metrics UI**

**Metric Card Component**: `/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/components/MetricCard.tsx`
- Simple card with icon, label, value, optional sub-label

**Available Metrics** (`/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/config/metrics.ts`):
1. **distance** (📏) - Total distance
2. **avg_pace** (⏱️) - Average pace in min/km
3. **avg_hr** (❤️) - Average heart rate in bpm
4. **workouts** (🏋️) - Workout count
5. **total_time** (🕐) - Total duration
6. **best_pace** (🚀) - Best pace
7. **elevation** (⛰️) - Total elevation in meters

**Charts & Visualizations**:
- **WeekChart** (`/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/components/WeekChart.tsx`):
  - Bar chart using Recharts
  - 7-day breakdown of km per day
  - Color-coded: green if >70% of max day, blue otherwise
  - No HR visualization currently

- **PeriodComparison** (`/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/components/PeriodComparison.tsx`):
  - Compares current month vs previous month by day-of-month
  - Shows absolute changes (not percentages):
    - Distance (km)
    - Workout count
    - Average pace (minutes:seconds format)
  - Improved/worsened indicators (▲/▼)

---

### 5. **Workout List/History**

**Files**:
- `/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/screens/History.tsx` - Full monthly history view
- `/c/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/components/WorkoutRow.tsx` - Reusable row component

**Data Shown Per Workout**:
- Badge (type indicator)
- Name
- Date (formatted)
- Distance (using manual_distance if available)
- Pace (calculated from time/distance, prefers manual values)
- GPS anomaly warning (⚠️)
- Verification status (✅)

**History Screen Features**:
- Month/year selector with navigation
- Configurable metric widgets (add/remove/reorder)
- Drag-and-drop reordering (desktop and touch)
- Widget settings modal
- All workouts for selected month listed below metrics

**Stats Calculation** (`useWorkoutHistory` hook):
- Aggregates stats for selected month
- Caches per month in localStorage
- Calculates from Workout[] array

---

### 6. **Data Structures & HR Integration**

**Workout Interface** (from `useWorkouts.ts`):
```typescript
interface Workout {
  id: string;
  strava_id: string;
  name: string;
  distance: number;
  moving_time: number;
  average_pace: number;
  average_heartrate: number | null;  // HR data
  max_heartrate: number | null;       // HR data
  date: string;
  type: string;
  splits: string | null;
  is_suspicious?: boolean;
  user_verified?: boolean;
  manual_distance?: number | null;
  manual_moving_time?: number | null;
}
```

**Stats Interface**:
```typescript
interface Stats {
  totalDistance: number;
  totalTime: number;
  avgPace: number;
  avgHeartrate: number;  // Includes HR metric
  bestPace: number;
  totalElevation: number;
  workoutCount: number;
}
```

---

### 7. **Key UI Patterns & Components**

**Modal System**:
- Overlay with React Portal
- Closing animation (300ms)
- Click-outside dismissal
- Used for: goals, records, pace zones, parameters, widget settings

**Navigation Screens**:
- Home (dashboard with widgets)
- History (monthly view)
- Profile (all-time stats, physical params, pace zones, records, goals)
- WorkoutDetail (individual workout deep dive)

**Widget System**:
- Configurable on Home, History, Profile screens
- Drag-to-reorder (with touch support)
- Add/remove from modal
- Persisted in localStorage by screen

---

### 8. **Existing HR-Related UI Elements**

1. **WorkoutDetail**: ❤️ metric card showing avg/max HR
2. **MetricCard**: `avg_hr` widget available in all dashboard screens
3. **Splits View**: HR data column (heartrate field per split)
4. **Stats**: `avgHeartrate` aggregated metric calculated from workouts
5. **Profile**: HR widget available in all-time stats section

---

### Summary of File Locations

```
/client/src/
├── screens/
│   ├── WorkoutDetail.tsx          (Main workout view with HR, splits)
│   ├── History.tsx                (Monthly workout list)
│   ├── Home.tsx                   (Dashboard)
│   ├── Profile.tsx                (Profile overview)
│   └── profile/
│       ├── GoalsSection.tsx       (Goals with breakdown modal)
│       ├── PaceZonesSection.tsx   (VDOT zones with details)
│       ├── RecordsSection.tsx     (Personal records)
│       └── SettingsModal.tsx      (Settings)
├── components/
│   ├── WorkoutRow.tsx             (Workout list item)
│   ├── MetricCard.tsx             (Metric display card)
│   ├── WeekChart.tsx              (Weekly bar chart)
│   ├── PeriodComparison.tsx       (Month-over-month comparison)
│   ├── GoalProgressMini.tsx       (Goal preview)
│   ├── MacroPlanView.tsx          (Plan visualization)
│   ├── MacroPlanTimeline.tsx      (Timeline view)
│   ├── PlanRow.tsx                (Plan item)
│   └── ChatMessage.tsx            (Chat UI)
├── hooks/
│   └── useWorkouts.ts             (Data fetching logic)
└── config/
    └── metrics.ts                 (Metric definitions)
```

4. **Stats**: `avgHeartrate` aggregated metric calculated from workouts
5. **Profile**: HR widget available in all-time stats section

---

### Summary of File Locations

```
/client/src/
├── screens/
│   ├── WorkoutDetail.tsx          (Main workout view with HR, splits)
│   ├── History.tsx                (Monthly workout list)
│   ├── Home.tsx                   (Dashboard)
│   ├── Profile.tsx                (Profile overview)
│   └── profile/
│       ├── GoalsSection.tsx       (Goals with breakdown modal)
│       ├── PaceZonesSection.tsx   (VDOT zones with details)
│       ├── RecordsSection.tsx     (Personal records)
│       └── SettingsModal.tsx      (Settings)
├── components/
│   ├── WorkoutRow.tsx             (Workout list item)
│   ├── MetricCard.tsx             (Metric display card)
│   ├── WeekChart.tsx              (Weekly bar chart)
│   ├── PeriodComparison.tsx       (Month-over-month comparison)
│   ├── GoalProgressMini.tsx       (Goal preview)
│   ├── MacroPlanView.tsx          (Plan visualization)
│   ├── MacroPlanTimeline.tsx      (Timeline view)
│   ├── PlanRow.tsx                (Plan item)
│   └── ChatMessage.tsx            (Chat UI)
├── hooks/
│   └── useWorkouts.ts             (Data fetching logic)
└── config/
    └── metrics.ts                 (Metric definitions)
```

Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me create a final summary document:

Based on my thorough exploration of the Runwise project, here's a comprehensive report on the **Heart Rate (HR) data flow**:

## HR Data Flow Summary

### 1. **HR Data from Strava** (`/server/routes/strava.js`)

**How HR is fetched:**
- Lines 112-135: `parseActivity()` function extracts HR from Strava activity objects:
  - `average_heartrate` (from Strava: `activity.average_heartrate`)
  - `max_heartrate` (from Strava: `activity.max_heartrate`)
  - **1km splits with HR**: Lines 128-135 parse `splits_metric` and extract `s.average_heartrate` per kilometer

- Lines 152-239: `calculate500mSplits()` function computes 500m splits from GPS streams
  - Lines 214-225: Averages heartrate data for each split from `hrArr` (heartrate stream)
  - Returns splits with `heartrate` field per 500m segment

- **Webhook processing** (Lines 778-865): On new activity creation, webhook handler calls `fetchAndSave500mSplits()` to pre-compute 500m splits with HR data fire-and-forget

**Endpoints:**
- `POST /api/strava/sync-splits-500/:workoutId` (Lines 631-706): Fetches streams with keys `['distance','time','heartrate']` and computes 500m splits with HR
- Background sync runs via `runSplitsSync()` (Lines 421-502): Updates workouts with splits that include HR

### 2. **HR Fields in Database Schema** (`/supabase_schema.sql`)

**Workouts table columns:**
- Line 27: `average_heartrate REAL` – Average HR for entire workout
- Line 28: `max_heartrate REAL` – Max HR during workout
- Line 31: `splits JSONB` – 1km splits with HR per km (lines 128-135 in strava.js show structure: `{km, time, pace, distance, heartrate, elevation}`)
- Line 59: `splits_500m JSONB` – 500m splits with HR (structure from line 232 in strava.js: `{km, time, pace, distance, heartrate}`)

**No dedicated HR storage columns** for:
- Resting HR (max_heartrate_baseline)
- User's max HR setting
- HR zones (calculated dynamically only, not stored)

### 3. **Splits Data Structure**

**1km splits** (stored in `splits` column):
```javascript
// From lines 128-135, 455-462 in strava.js
{
  km: 1,
  time: moving_time_seconds,
  pace: time_per_km_sec,
  distance: distance_m,
  heartrate: avg_hr_for_km || null,  // <-- HR included
  elevation: elevation_difference_m
}
```

**500m splits** (stored in `splits_500m` column):
```javascript
// From line 232 in strava.js
{
  km: 0.5,  // cumulative distance in km
  time: rounded_time_sec,
  pace: rounded_pace_sec_per_km,
  distance: rounded_split_distance_m,
  heartrate: rounded_avg_hr || null  // <-- HR included
}
```

Both already contain HR data when available from Strava streams.

### 4. **VDOT Calculation** (`/server/routes/ai/vdot.js`)

**Does NOT use HR data.** Uses only:
- Distance and moving_time → calculates velocity
- `timeToPctVO2max()` function (lines 15-20): Estimates %VO2max from **duration only** (exponential decay model)
- No HR input → No HR zones calculated from HR

The algorithm (lines 41-57):
```
velocity (m/min) = distance / time_minutes
VO2 = -4.60 + 0.182258*velocity + 0.000104*velocity²
%VO2max = f(time_minutes)  // NO HR involved
VDOT = VO2 / %VO2max
```

### 5. **AI Context** (`/server/routes/ai/context.js`)

**HR data passed to AI:**
- Line 87: `getWorkoutsContext()` includes `average_heartrate` in 3-month summary
- Line 97: Returns `heartrate: w.average_heartrate || '—'` for each workout
- Line 109, 131: `getMonthlySummaryContext()` calculates `avg_heartrate` from workouts with HR data
- Line 145: `avg_heartrate: hrs.length ? Math.round(avg) : null` – weighted average

**Example context sent to AI** (lines 139-149):
```javascript
{
  period: '30 days',
  workouts_count: 12,
  total_km: 85.5,
  total_time_min: 480,
  avg_pace: '5:30/km',
  avg_heartrate: 155,  // <-- Only this ONE number
  total_elevation: 240,
  type_breakdown: {...}
}
```

**AI Prompt usage** (`/server/routes/ai/prompts.js`):
- Line 137: Monthly summary includes `avg HR ${summary.avg_heartrate}` (one-liner)
- Line 1028: Comment only: *"Consider age when recommending heart rate zones and recovery"* (aspirational, not implemented)
- **NO HR zones are mentioned** in AI context – only pace zones from VDOT

### 6. **Predictions Algorithm** (`/server/routes/workouts/predictions.js`)

**Does NOT use HR data.** Uses:
- Distance + time → **pace-based Riegel formula** (line 198)
- `riegelExp` (line 178): Varies based on `avgPace` (5-pace dependent)
- Best efforts (clean splits from Strava)
- **HR is completely absent** from prediction logic

Riegel formula (line 198):
```javascript
predictedTime = baseEffort * (targetDist / baseEffort_dist) ^ riegelExp
// NO HR in this formula
```

### 7. **Profile/User Settings** (`/server/routes/profile.js` & `supabase_schema.sql`)

**Available user fields** (lines 12-14, 40-41 in profile.js):
- `age` – Yes
- `height_cm` – Yes
- `weight_kg` – Yes
- `gender` – Yes
- `ai_preferences` – JSON object for coach personality

**NOT available:**
- `max_heartrate` (user's max HR)
- `resting_heartrate` (baseline HR)
- `hr_reserve` (Karvonen zone calculation)

No endpoints to set or retrieve max HR or resting HR.

---

## Key Findings

| Aspect | Status | Details |
|--------|--------|---------|
| **HR data import** | ✅ Yes | From Strava average/max HR and streams (1km & 500m splits) |
| **HR storage** | ✅ Yes | `workouts.average_heartrate`, `workouts.max_heartrate`, in splits JSONB |
| **HR to AI** | ⚠️ Partial | Only **monthly average HR** in context, not per-workout or zones |
| **HR for VDOT** | ❌ No | VDOT uses time+distance only (Daniels formula), no HR input |
| **HR zones** | ❌ No | Only pace zones exist (from VDOT), no HR zones calculated |
| **HR for predictions** | ❌ No | Riegel formula uses pace only |
| **User max HR setting** | ❌ No | Not stored anywhere |
| **User resting HR** | ❌ No | Not stored anywhere |

**Opportunity: HR data is collected but underutilized** – could enhance VDOT calculations, provide HR zone recommendations, and personalize AI coaching with HR-based insights.

input.prompt:
Design an implementation plan for integrating heart rate (HR) data into the Runwise running app. The key requirement is that EVERYTHING must work both WITH and WITHOUT HR data — HR is an optional enhancement, not a requirement.

## Current State

### HR Data Already Available:
- `workouts.average_heartrate` (REAL) and `workouts.max_heartrate` (REAL) columns exist in DB
- 1km splits have `heartrate` field per km (from Strava `splits_metric`)
- 500m splits have `heartrate` field per 500m segment (calculated from Strava HR streams)
- WorkoutDetail.tsx already shows avg/max HR in metric cards
- AI context already passes monthly `avg_heartrate` (one number only)

### What's Missing:
- No user max_heartrate or resting_heartrate stored in profile
- No HR zones (only pace zones from VDOT exist)
- VDOT calculation (`/server/routes/ai/vdot.js`) uses only time+distance, no HR
- Predictions (`/server/routes/workouts/predictions.js`) use only Riegel pace formula, no HR
- AI context passes only one aggregated avg HR number, not per-workout HR or zones
- No Cardiac Efficiency metric
- No Aerobic Decoupling metric
- No TRIMP/load tracking

## Features to Implement (in priority order):

### Feature 1: Enhanced HR data in AI context
**Goal:** Give the AI coach much richer HR info so it can give better advice.
**Where:** `/server/routes/ai/context.js` and `/server/routes/ai/prompts.js`
**What to add:**
- Per-workout: avg HR, max HR alongside existing pace/distance
- HR trend over recent weeks (is avg HR going down at same pace? = improving fitness)
- If user has max HR set: include %HRmax context
- Cardiac Efficiency trend (pace/HR ratio) over last 4 weeks
**Guard:** All HR fields are optional — if `average_heartrate` is null, omit HR info for that workout

### Feature 2: User max HR and resting HR in profile
**Goal:** Allow user to optionally set their max HR and resting HR for better zone calculations
**Where:** 
- DB: Add `max_heartrate_user` and `resting_heartrate` to profiles table (or use existing columns)
- Server: `/server/routes/profile.js` — add fields to get/update
- Client: `/client/src/screens/profile/SettingsModal.tsx` — add optional HR input fields
**Auto-estimation:** If user doesn't set max HR manually, estimate from `220 - age` (age is already in profile). If user has actual max HR from workouts, suggest that value.
**Guard:** These fields are NULL by default, everything works without them

### Feature 3: Cardiac Efficiency (CE) metric and trend
**Goal:** Show user their fitness trend over time — pace-to-HR ratio
**Formula:** CE = pace (sec/km) / avg_heartrate. Lower = better (faster pace at lower HR)
**Where:**
- Server: New utility function, used in AI context and optionally in stats
- Client: Could be shown in PeriodComparison or as a new metric widget
**Guard:** Only calculate when average_heartrate is not null

### Feature 4: HR-adjusted race predictions
**Goal:** If user ran a race at low %HRmax, they have more potential — adjust Riegel prediction
**Where:** `/server/routes/workouts/predictions.js`
**Logic:** 
- If workout has HR data AND user has max HR: calculate %HRmax during the effort
- If %HRmax < 85% → the effort was submaximal, multiply Riegel estimate by a small correction factor (e.g., 0.97-0.99)
- If %HRmax > 95% → near-maximal, the time is close to true potential (no adjustment)
**Guard:** If no HR data or no max HR, skip correction entirely — existing Riegel works as-is

### Feature 5: Aerobic Decoupling per long run
**Goal:** Show how well user's aerobic system held up during long runs
**Formula:** Compare avg HR of first half vs second half at similar pace. drift% = (HR2-HR1)/HR1*100
**Where:**
- Server: Calculate from 500m splits (which have HR) when splits exist
- Client: Show in WorkoutDetail for runs > 10km with HR data
- AI context: Include for recent long runs
**Guard:** Only show when 500m splits with HR are available AND run is > 10km

### Feature 6: TRIMP (Training Impulse) for weekly load
**Goal:** Simple weekly load indicator based on HR and duration
**Formula:** TRIMP = duration(min) × avg_HR_fraction × intensity_factor
**Where:**
- Server: Calculate per workout, aggregate per week
- Client: Show as simple indicator (low/medium/high) or number
- AI context: Include weekly TRIMP trend
**Guard:** Only for workouts with HR. For those without, show load based on distance/time only

## Existing Code Patterns to Follow:

### Server patterns:
- API routes in `/server/routes/` with Express router
- Supabase client for DB queries
- Auth middleware: `const userId = req.user?.id`
- AI context functions return formatted strings
- VDOT module exports pure functions

### Client patterns:
- React functional components with hooks
- CSS in separate `.css` files (no CSS modules)
- Translations via `react-i18next` with `t('key')` 
- MetricCard component for displaying stats
- Modal system with overlay + portal
- localStorage caching for performance
- Optional data shown conditionally: `{value && <Component />}`

### Key files to modify:
- `/server/routes/ai/context.js` (lines 87-149 — workout context and monthly summary)
- `/server/routes/ai/prompts.js` (lines 130-140 — monthly summary formatting)
- `/server/routes/ai/vdot.js` (pure VDOT calculation)
- `/server/routes/workouts/predictions.js` (Riegel predictions)
- `/server/routes/profile.js` (profile CRUD)
- `/client/src/screens/profile/SettingsModal.tsx` (settings UI)
- `/client/src/screens/profile/PaceZonesSection.tsx` (zones display)
- `/client/src/screens/WorkoutDetail.tsx` (workout view)
- `/client/src/components/PeriodComparison.tsx` (month comparison)
- `/client/src/config/metrics.ts` (metric definitions)

Design a concrete implementation plan with specific steps, file changes, and code approaches. Group by feature. Make it actionable.

content.text:
Now I have all the information needed. Let me construct the comprehensive implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: Heart Rate Integration for Runwise

This plan is organized into six features, ordered by priority and dependency. The foundational principle throughout: every HR-dependent feature must degrade gracefully to a no-HR state via null checks.

---

### Feature 1: Enhanced HR Data in AI Context

**Goal:** Enrich the system prompt and AI tools with per-workout HR, HR trends, cardiac efficiency trend, and optional %HRmax.

**Current state:**
- `context.js:97` includes `heartrate: w.average_heartrate || '—'` in per-workout context (used in `getWorkoutsContext`)
- `context.js:131` computes a single `avg_heartrate` for 30-day summary
- `prompts.js:137` emits `avg HR ${summary.avg_heartrate}` as one number in the compact monthly summary
- `chat.js:59-68` loads `monthlySummary` and passes it to `buildChatSystemPrompt`
- The system prompt at `prompts.js:1194-1195` shows the monthly summary as a single line

**Changes needed:**

**File: `/server/routes/ai/context.js`**

1. **Modify `getMonthlySummaryContext` (lines 103-149):** Also select `max_heartrate` in the DB query (line 109). Compute additional HR aggregates alongside existing ones:
   - `max_heartrate_peak`: the highest `max_heartrate` across all workouts in the period
   - `hr_workouts_count`: count of workouts with HR data vs total (to show coverage)

2. **Add new function `getHRTrendContext(userId)`:** Query last 4 weeks of workouts, group by week. For each week, compute avg HR and avg pace for runs with HR. Return an array of `{ week, avgHR, avgPace, cardiacEfficiency }` where CE = avg_pace / avg_HR. This is compact enough for the prompt (4 lines). Guard: return null if fewer than 2 weeks have HR data.

3. **Add new function `getCardiacEfficiencyTrend(userId)`:** This can be part of `getHRTrendContext` -- just the CE values. CE = pace_sec_per_km / average_heartrate. Lower CE = better fitness (faster at lower HR). Compute for last 4 weeks. Guard: skip workouts without `average_heartrate`.

4. **Modify `getUserProfile` (line 254-261):** Add `max_heartrate_user, resting_heartrate` to the select query. This depends on Feature 2 DB changes, but the select can be added now with graceful null handling.

**File: `/server/routes/ai/prompts.js`**

5. **Modify `formatMonthlySummaryCompact` (lines 125-147):** After the existing `avg HR` line, append `max HR peak {value}` if available. Append HR coverage: `(HR: {count}/{total} workouts)`.

6. **Add new function `formatHRTrendBlock(hrTrend, userProfile, lang)`:** Formats the weekly HR trend for the system prompt. If user has `max_heartrate_user`, include %HRmax for the avg HR of each week. Example output:
   ```
   HR TREND (last 4 weeks):
   Week 1: avg HR 152, avg pace 5:30/km, CE 2.17
   Week 2: avg HR 149, avg pace 5:28/km, CE 2.21 (improving)
   ```
   Guard: return empty string if hrTrend is null.

7. **Modify `formatProfileForAI` (lines 234-248):** If `profile.max_heartrate_user` is set, include `Max HR: {value}`. If `profile.resting_heartrate` is set, include `Resting HR: {value}`.

8. **Modify `buildChatSystemPrompt` (line 702):** Add `hrTrend` parameter. Insert `formatHRTrendBlock(hrTrend, userProfile, lang)` after the monthly summary section (~line 1208). This is a new block that only appears when HR data exists.

**File: `/server/routes/ai/chat.js`**

9. **Modify `loadChatContext` (line 46-133):** Call the new `getHRTrendContext(userId)` in the Promise.all array. Pass the result as a new parameter to `buildChatSystemPrompt`.

**File: `/server/routes/ai/workout.js`**

10. **Modify workout analysis prompt (line 84-94):** Already includes avg/max HR. Add: if user profile has `max_heartrate_user`, compute and include `%HRmax: {avg_hr / max_hr * 100}%` in the prompt. Guard: only if both values exist.

**Token budget consideration:** The HR trend block adds ~200-300 tokens to the system prompt. This is within the budget since the existing prompt is already large. The block only appears when HR data exists, so users without HR monitors see no change.

---

### Feature 2: User Max HR and Resting HR in Profile

**Goal:** Allow optional max HR and resting HR storage for zone calculations.

**Changes needed:**

**Database (Supabase):**

1. **Add columns to `users` table:**
   ```sql
   ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN max_heartrate_user INTEGER DEFAULT NULL;
   ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN resting_heartrate INTEGER DEFAULT NULL;
   ```
   Both nullable. The naming `max_heartrate_user` distinguishes from per-workout `max_heartrate`.

**File: `/server/routes/profile.js`**

2. **Modify GET handler (lines 8-20):** Add `max_heartrate_user, resting_heartrate` to the select query on line 12. The result will include these as null for existing users.

3. **Modify PUT handler (lines 24-46):** Accept `max_heartrate_user` and `resting_heartrate` in `req.body`. Add them to `updateFields` if present. Add to the return select on line 39. Validate: max_heartrate_user must be 100-230 if provided, resting_heartrate must be 30-100 if provided.

**File: `/client/src/api/api.ts`**

4. **Modify `profile.update` type (line 118):** Add `max_heartrate_user?: number | null; resting_heartrate?: number | null` to the data parameter type.

**File: `/client/src/screens/profile/SettingsModal.tsx`**

This is the wrong place. The profile physical params (age, height, weight, gender) are edited in a params modal within `Profile.tsx`, not in `SettingsModal.tsx`. Let me verify.

**File: `/client/src/screens/Profile.tsx`**

5. **Add state variables** for `maxHR` and `restingHR` alongside the existing `age`, `height`, `weight`, `gender` states (~line 70 area). Initialize from profile API response (alongside existing field mapping, around line 219-223).

6. **Add input fields** in the physical params modal (the one opened by the edit button). Two new optional fields:
   - "Max HR" with placeholder suggesting `220 - age` estimate if age is set
   - "Resting HR" with placeholder "optional"
   Both use the same input pattern as existing height/weight fields.

7. **Modify `handleSaveProfile`** (~line 240-248): Include `max_heartrate_user` and `resting_heartrate` in the `profileApi.update()` call.

8. **Auto-estimation logic (client-side):** When user opens the params modal and max HR is not set:
   - If `age` is available, show helper text: "Estimated: {220 - age} bpm"
   - If workouts have recorded max HR values, suggest the highest observed max HR from workouts (requires a separate small API call or passing it from stats)

**File: `/client/src/i18n/locales/ru.json`, `en.json`, `uk.json`**

9. **Add translation keys:**
   - `profile.maxHeartRate`: "Max HR" / "Макс. пульс" / "Макс. пульс"
   - `profile.restingHeartRate`: "Resting HR" / "Пульс покоя" / "Пульс спокою"
   - `profile.maxHrEstimate`: "Estimated: {{value}} bpm" (etc.)
   - `profile.maxHrHint`: "Used for HR zone calculation" (etc.)

---

### Feature 3: Cardiac Efficiency Metric and Trend

**Goal:** Expose CE = pace / avg_HR as a trend metric.

**Changes needed:**

**File: `/server/routes/ai/context.js`** (already covered in Feature 1, step 2-3)

The CE computation is part of `getHRTrendContext`. The pure function:
```javascript
function calcCardiacEfficiency(paceSecPerKm, avgHeartrate) {
  if (!paceSecPerKm || !avgHeartrate || avgHeartrate === 0) return null;
  return Math.round((paceSecPerKm / avgHeartrate) * 100) / 100;
}
```
This should be a utility exported from `context.js` for reuse.

**File: `/server/routes/workouts/workouts.js`**

1. **Modify the comparison endpoint** (lines 207-270): The `calcStats` inner function currently computes distance, workoutCount, avgPace. Add `avgCE` to the stats: for workouts with both pace and HR, compute the average CE. Return it alongside other comparison metrics. Guard: `avgCE` is null if no workouts have HR.

**File: `/client/src/components/PeriodComparison.tsx`**

2. **Extend `PeriodStats` interface** (line 7-11): Add `avgCE?: number | null`.

3. **Add CE to the metrics array** (around line 52): A new metric entry for CE, shown only when both current and previous have non-null CE. Use the existing conditional pattern. CE improvement = current CE is lower (faster pace per heartbeat). Format as decimal with 2 places.

**File: `/client/src/config/metrics.ts`**

4. **Add a new `cardiac_efficiency` metric** to `ALL_METRICS` array. It needs `avgHeartrate` and `avgPace` to both be nonzero in the Stats. Formula: `s.avgPace / s.avgHeartrate` displayed as decimal. Guard: show "---" when either is missing. This metric would be available for dashboard/profile/history widget selection.

**File: `/client/src/hooks/useWorkouts.ts`**

5. **Extend the `Stats` interface** (line 22-30): Consider adding `avgCE?: number` computed in `calcStats`. Or keep it derived on the client from existing stats.

**Translation files:**

6. Add keys: `metrics.cardiacEfficiency`, `comparison.cardiacEfficiency`, `units.ceUnit` (or just show the number).

---

### Feature 4: HR-Adjusted Race Predictions

**Goal:** Correct Riegel predictions when a reference effort was submaximal.

**Changes needed:**

**File: `/server/routes/workouts/predictions.js`**

This is the most self-contained change. The file currently uses Riegel formula at lines 197-201 and best efforts at lines 239-271.

1. **Add HR correction function** near the top of the file:
   ```javascript
   function hrCorrectionFactor(avgHR, maxHR) {
     if (!avgHR || !maxHR || maxHR <= 0) return 1.0; // no correction
     const pctHRmax = avgHR / maxHR;
     if (pctHRmax >= 0.95) return 1.0;    // near-maximal, no adjustment
     if (pctHRmax >= 0.90) return 0.99;   // slight underperformance
     if (pctHRmax >= 0.85) return 0.98;   // moderate submaximal
     if (pctHRmax >= 0.80) return 0.97;   // clearly submaximal
     return 0.96;                          // very easy effort
   }
   ```
   This multiplier is applied to Riegel time estimates: `rTime * hrCorrectionFactor(...)`.

2. **Load user's max HR:** At the beginning of the handler (after `req.user.id`), fetch `max_heartrate_user` from the users table. If null, try `220 - age` if age exists. If neither, skip HR correction entirely.

3. **Load workout HR data:** The query at line 42-47 already selects workouts. Needs to also select `average_heartrate` (currently not in the select). Add it.

4. **Apply correction to Riegel estimates:** In the loop at lines 193-216, when building `allRiegelResults`, if the source workout has `average_heartrate` and user maxHR is known, multiply the Riegel time by `hrCorrectionFactor(workout.average_heartrate, userMaxHR)`. This makes the prediction slightly faster for submaximal reference efforts.

5. **Add to breakdown:** Include `hrAdjusted: true/false` and `pctHRmax` in the breakdown data so the UI can show why a prediction was adjusted.

6. **Guard:** The entire HR correction path is skipped if `average_heartrate` is null for the workout OR `maxHR` is not determinable. Existing behavior is unchanged.

**File: `/client/src/screens/profile/GoalsSection.tsx`** (optional UI enhancement)

7. If breakdown includes `hrAdjusted: true`, show a small note: "Adjusted for submaximal effort ({pctHRmax}% HRmax)".

---

### Feature 5: Aerobic Decoupling Per Long Run

**Goal:** Compare first-half vs second-half HR drift during long runs.

**Changes needed:**

**File: `/server/routes/workouts/workouts.js`** (or a new utility file)

1. **Add utility function `calcAerobicDecoupling(splits500m)`:**
   ```javascript
   function calcAerobicDecoupling(splits500m) {
     if (!splits500m || splits500m.length < 4) return null;
     
     const splitsWithHR = splits500m.filter(s => s.heartrate && s.heartrate > 0);
     if (splitsWithHR.length < 4) return null;
     
     const mid = Math.floor(splitsWithHR.length / 2);
     const firstHalf = splitsWithHR.slice(0, mid);
     const secondHalf = splitsWithHR.slice(mid);
     
     const avgHR1 = firstHalf.reduce((s, sp) => s + sp.heartrate, 0) / firstHalf.length;
     const avgHR2 = secondHalf.reduce((s, sp) => s + sp.heartrate, 0) / secondHalf.length;
     
     if (avgHR1 === 0) return null;
     
     const drift = ((avgHR2 - avgHR1) / avgHR1) * 100;
     return { drift: Math.round(drift * 10) / 10, avgHR1: Math.round(avgHR1), avgHR2: Math.round(avgHR2) };
   }
   ```
   Interpretation: drift < 5% = excellent aerobic fitness; 5-10% = acceptable; >10% = needs more base work.

**File: `/server/routes/ai/tools.js`**

2. **Modify `get_workout_details` tool handler** (around line 174-220): After retrieving splits_500m for the workout, if the run is >10km and has 500m splits with HR, compute aerobic decoupling and include it in the tool response. Guard: only compute if splits exist and have HR.

**File: `/server/routes/ai/workout.js`**

3. **Modify workout analysis** (lines 25-97): When analyzing a long run (>10km) with splits_500m, fetch the 500m splits from the workout record, compute decoupling, and include it in the analysis prompt. This gives the AI concrete data to comment on aerobic endurance.

**File: `/client/src/screens/WorkoutDetail.tsx`**

4. **Add Aerobic Decoupling card:** After the splits section, for runs >10km that have 500m splits with HR data, show a "Decoupling" metric. Compute it client-side from the 500m splits data that is already fetched. Display:
   - The drift percentage
   - Color coding: green (<5%), yellow (5-10%), red (>10%)
   - Brief interpretation text

5. **Guard:** Only render when `workout.distance >= 10000` AND 500m splits with HR are loaded AND at least 4 splits have HR values.

**File: `/server/routes/ai/context.js`**

6. **New function `getRecentDecouplingData(userId)`:** Query recent long runs (>10km, last 30 days) that have `splits_500m`. Parse their 500m splits, compute decoupling for each. Return as compact array for AI context. Guard: return null if no qualifying runs.

**Translation files:**

7. Add keys: `workout.aerobicDecoupling`, `workout.decouplingGood`, `workout.decouplingModerate`, `workout.decouplingHigh`, `workout.hrDrift`.

---

### Feature 6: TRIMP (Training Impulse) for Weekly Load

**Goal:** Simple weekly load indicator based on HR and duration.

This is the lowest priority and most complex feature. It should be implemented last.

**Changes needed:**

**File: `/server/routes/ai/context.js`** (or new utility file)

1. **Add TRIMP calculation function:**
   ```javascript
   function calcTRIMP(durationMin, avgHR, restingHR, maxHR, gender) {
     if (!durationMin || !avgHR) return null;
     // If no resting/max HR, use simplified TRIMP
     if (!restingHR || !maxHR) {
       // Simplified: duration * HR fraction (HR/180 as rough proxy)
       return Math.round(durationMin * (avgHR / 180));
     }
     // Banister's TRIMP with HRR (heart rate reserve)
     const hrr = (avgHR - restingHR) / (maxHR - restingHR);
     const clampedHRR = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, hrr));
     const genderFactor = gender === 'female' ? 1.67 : 1.92;
     const exponent = genderFactor * clampedHRR;
     return Math.round(durationMin * clampedHRR * 0.64 * Math.exp(exponent));
   }
   ```
   Guard: returns null if duration or avgHR is missing.

2. **Add weekly TRIMP aggregation function `getWeeklyTRIMP(userId)`:** Query last 4 weeks of workouts. For each workout with HR, compute TRIMP. Aggregate per week. For workouts without HR, compute a simplified load: `duration_min * (avg_pace_ratio)` where pace_ratio is based on workout type. Return: `{ weeks: [{trimp: N, totalMinutes: M, workoutsWithHR: K, totalWorkouts: T}], trend: 'increasing'|'stable'|'decreasing' }`.

**File: `/server/routes/ai/prompts.js`**

3. **Add `formatTRIMPBlock(trimpData, lang)`:** Format weekly TRIMP for system prompt. Example:
   ```
   TRAINING LOAD (TRIMP, last 4 weeks):
   Week 1: 320 (4 workouts, 3 with HR)
   Week 2: 280 (3 workouts)
   Trend: stable
   ```

4. **Wire into `buildChatSystemPrompt`:** Add as new parameter and insert block.

**File: `/server/routes/ai/chat.js`**

5. **Call `getWeeklyTRIMP` in `loadChatContext`** and pass to system prompt builder.

**Client-side (optional, can be deferred):**

6. Show weekly TRIMP as a simple indicator in the dashboard or period comparison. Could be a new metric in `metrics.ts`: id `weekly_trimp`, shown as a number or a qualitative label (Low < 200, Medium 200-400, High > 400).

---

### Implementation Order and Dependencies

```
Feature 2 (DB + Profile) ─── first, because Features 1,3,4,6 reference max_heartrate_user
    │
Feature 1 (AI Context) ───── second, provides foundation for AI to use HR data
    │
Feature 3 (Cardiac Efficiency) ── can be done in parallel with Feature 4
Feature 4 (HR-adjusted predictions) ── can be done in parallel with Feature 3
    │
Feature 5 (Aerobic Decoupling) ── standalone, requires only existing 500m splits
Feature 6 (TRIMP) ────────── last, most complex, lowest priority
```

### Key Design Decisions

1. **No new DB tables.** All new user fields go on the existing `users` table (2 columns). TRIMP and CE are computed on-the-fly, not stored.

2. **All guards are null-propagating.** Every function returns null when HR data is missing. Every UI component uses `{value && <Component />}` pattern. Every prompt section is omitted when data is null.

3. **Simplified TRIMP fallback.** When resting/max HR are unknown, TRIMP uses a simplified formula rather than being unavailable entirely. This gives partial value to users who have workout HR but not profile HR settings.

4. **HR correction for predictions is conservative.** The correction factor ranges from 0.96 to 1.0. This means at most a 4% improvement for very submaximal efforts. This avoids over-promising.

5. **Aerobic decoupling requires 500m splits.** Not all workouts have them (only those where the user triggered the 500m split fetch from Strava). The feature is only shown when data exists.

6. **Token budget for AI prompt.** New HR blocks add at most ~500 tokens to the system prompt. They only appear when HR data exists. Given the existing prompt is already very large (the macro plan section alone is thousands of tokens), this is acceptable.

### Critical Files for Implementation
- `C:/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/server/routes/ai/context.js` -- central hub for all HR context functions (getHRTrendContext, CE calculation, weekly TRIMP, decoupling aggregation)
- `C:/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/server/routes/ai/prompts.js` -- formatting all new HR blocks for the system prompt and wiring them into buildChatSystemPrompt
- `C:/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/server/routes/profile.js` -- adding max_heartrate_user and resting_heartrate to profile CRUD
- `C:/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/server/routes/workouts/predictions.js` -- HR-adjusted Riegel correction logic
- `C:/Users/Тима/Desktop/Runwise/client/src/screens/WorkoutDetail.tsx` -- aerobic decoupling display in workout view

toolUseResult.prompt:
Design an implementation plan for integrating heart rate (HR) data into the Runwise running app. The key requirement is that EVERYTHING must work both WITH and WITHOUT HR data — HR is an optional enhancement, not a requirement.

## Current State

### HR Data Already Available:
- `workouts.average_heartrate` (REAL) and `workouts.max_heartrate` (REAL) columns exist in DB
- 1km splits have `heartrate` field per km (from Strava `splits_metric`)
- 500m splits have `heartrate` field per 500m segment (calculated from Strava HR streams)
- WorkoutDetail.tsx already shows avg/max HR in metric cards
- AI context already passes monthly `avg_heartrate` (one number only)

### What's Missing:
- No user max_heartrate or resting_heartrate stored in profile
- No HR zones (only pace zones from VDOT exist)
- VDOT calculation (`/server/routes/ai/vdot.js`) uses only time+distance, no HR
- Predictions (`/server/routes/workouts/predictions.js`) use only Riegel pace formula, no HR
- AI context passes only one aggregated avg HR number, not per-workout HR or zones
- No Cardiac Efficiency metric
- No Aerobic Decoupling metric
- No TRIMP/load tracking

## Features to Implement (in priority order):

### Feature 1: Enhanced HR data in AI context
**Goal:** Give the AI coach much richer HR info so it can give better advice.
**Where:** `/server/routes/ai/context.js` and `/server/routes/ai/prompts.js`
**What to add:**
- Per-workout: avg HR, max HR alongside existing pace/distance
- HR trend over recent weeks (is avg HR going down at same pace? = improving fitness)
- If user has max HR set: include %HRmax context
- Cardiac Efficiency trend (pace/HR ratio) over last 4 weeks
**Guard:** All HR fields are optional — if `average_heartrate` is null, omit HR info for that workout

### Feature 2: User max HR and resting HR in profile
**Goal:** Allow user to optionally set their max HR and resting HR for better zone calculations
**Where:** 
- DB: Add `max_heartrate_user` and `resting_heartrate` to profiles table (or use existing columns)
- Server: `/server/routes/profile.js` — add fields to get/update
- Client: `/client/src/screens/profile/SettingsModal.tsx` — add optional HR input fields
**Auto-estimation:** If user doesn't set max HR manually, estimate from `220 - age` (age is already in profile). If user has actual max HR from workouts, suggest that value.
**Guard:** These fields are NULL by default, everything works without them

### Feature 3: Cardiac Efficiency (CE) metric and trend
**Goal:** Show user their fitness trend over time — pace-to-HR ratio
**Formula:** CE = pace (sec/km) / avg_heartrate. Lower = better (faster pace at lower HR)
**Where:**
- Server: New utility function, used in AI context and optionally in stats
- Client: Could be shown in PeriodComparison or as a new metric widget
**Guard:** Only calculate when average_heartrate is not null

### Feature 4: HR-adjusted race predictions
**Goal:** If user ran a race at low %HRmax, they have more potential — adjust Riegel prediction
**Where:** `/server/routes/workouts/predictions.js`
**Logic:** 
- If workout has HR data AND user has max HR: calculate %HRmax during the effort
- If %HRmax < 85% → the effort was submaximal, multiply Riegel estimate by a small correction factor (e.g., 0.97-0.99)
- If %HRmax > 95% → near-maximal, the time is close to true potential (no adjustment)
**Guard:** If no HR data or no max HR, skip correction entirely — existing Riegel works as-is

### Feature 5: Aerobic Decoupling per long run
**Goal:** Show how well user's aerobic system held up during long runs
**Formula:** Compare avg HR of first half vs second half at similar pace. drift% = (HR2-HR1)/HR1*100
**Where:**
- Server: Calculate from 500m splits (which have HR) when splits exist
- Client: Show in WorkoutDetail for runs > 10km with HR data
- AI context: Include for recent long runs
**Guard:** Only show when 500m splits with HR are available AND run is > 10km

### Feature 6: TRIMP (Training Impulse) for weekly load
**Goal:** Simple weekly load indicator based on HR and duration
**Formula:** TRIMP = duration(min) × avg_HR_fraction × intensity_factor
**Where:**
- Server: Calculate per workout, aggregate per week
- Client: Show as simple indicator (low/medium/high) or number
- AI context: Include weekly TRIMP trend
**Guard:** Only for workouts with HR. For those without, show load based on distance/time only

## Existing Code Patterns to Follow:

### Server patterns:
- API routes in `/server/routes/` with Express router
- Supabase client for DB queries
- Auth middleware: `const userId = req.user?.id`
- AI context functions return formatted strings
- VDOT module exports pure functions

### Client patterns:
- React functional components with hooks
- CSS in separate `.css` files (no CSS modules)
- Translations via `react-i18next` with `t('key')` 
- MetricCard component for displaying stats
- Modal system with overlay + portal
- localStorage caching for performance
- Optional data shown conditionally: `{value && <Component />}`

### Key files to modify:
- `/server/routes/ai/context.js` (lines 87-149 — workout context and monthly summary)
- `/server/routes/ai/prompts.js` (lines 130-140 — monthly summary formatting)
- `/server/routes/ai/vdot.js` (pure VDOT calculation)
- `/server/routes/workouts/predictions.js` (Riegel predictions)
- `/server/routes/profile.js` (profile CRUD)
- `/client/src/screens/profile/SettingsModal.tsx` (settings UI)
- `/client/src/screens/profile/PaceZonesSection.tsx` (zones display)
- `/client/src/screens/WorkoutDetail.tsx` (workout view)
- `/client/src/components/PeriodComparison.tsx` (month comparison)
- `/client/src/config/metrics.ts` (metric definitions)

Design a concrete implementation plan with specific steps, file changes, and code approaches. Group by feature. Make it actionable.
